author: interdict
text:
Re-check task id under the per-task lock

Two concurrent bl create invocations can mint the same 4-hex id when their next_unique_id calls land in the same microsecond: task_exists runs unlocked, so both compute the same SHA1 prefix and both see the id unused. The per-task lock then serializes them on the same id, and without a re-check the second holder's save_task silently overwrites the first's task JSON — both bl create calls exit 0, but the loser's task is gone from disk and from HEAD.

Replace the bare mint+lock pattern with task_id::mint_and_lock: pick an id, take its per-task lock, re-check task_exists, and on collision drop the lock, bump the timestamp by 1 ms, and remint. The colliding loser observes the winner's file under the lock and mints a fresh id instead of overwriting. cmd_create's new lock guard drops inside the mutate closure so plugin::finish (apply_push_contributions retakes the same flock for any plugin subscribed to create) does not deadlock. review_deferred and sync_report adopt mint_and_lock too — the deferred gate-child path also gains a per-task lock it lacked before. Drop generate_task_id, no callers left after the switch.

Tested with a deterministic unit test that pre-holds the contested flock, lets a worker thread reach task_lock and block, saves the winner's file, then releases the lock — the worker's post-lock re-check must observe the file and remint past it.
